title: U.S. government restricts launch of new AI models from Anthropic and OpenAI
summary: Both Anthropic and OpenAI must obtain government approval to release their Mythos and GPT-5.6 models, respectively.

title: Microsoft extends free security updates for Windows 10 until October 2027
summary: The company extends support for Windows 10 due to slow adoption of Windows 11, offering two additional years of security patches.

title: AMD relaunches the Ryzen 7 5800X3D as a 10th Anniversary Edition
summary: The popular AM4 chip becomes available again after a special edition that resellers are already selling at high prices.

title: Apple will abandon the M6 Pro and M6 Max chips to focus on the AI-centric M7
summary: According to reports, the company will skip the high-end versions of the M6 and directly launch an AI-optimized M7 in 2027.

title: PlayStation removes 551 movies from user accounts
summary: Sony deletes digitally purchased content, reigniting the debate over digital content ownership.

title: Europe launches Eustella, its own AI chatbot as an alternative to ChatGPT and Claude
summary: The European Union presents a sovereign AI assistant with agent features, competing with US models.

title: Artificial intelligence is replacing junior and entry-level jobs
summary: Companies like Anthropic and SAP confirm they no longer need junior engineers and that a third of employers are replacing entry-level roles with AI.

title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of cloning its Claude model with 25,000 fake accounts
summary: The AI company reports the largest known distillation attack, calling for sanctions against the Chinese firm.

title: IBM unveils the 0.7-nanometer chip, the smallest and most powerful technology in the world
summary: The new node offers up to 50% more performance and 70% more energy efficiency compared to the 2 nm process.

title: Framework lowers prices on the Laptop 13 Pro amid Apple price increases
summary: The modular laptop company cuts its prices to compete with Macs, while warning of a possible increase in processor prices.

title: Rockstar reveals GTA VI price and release date for November
summary: GTA VI will arrive on November 19 with a country-specific price and promises to revolutionize the industry with AI and integrated social media.

title: Console industry faces PS5 sales decline, Xbox layoffs, and price increases
summary: PlayStation sales hit their lowest level in 26 years while Xbox raises prices and lays off former employees who reported retaliation.

title: Top technology deals from Amazon Prime Day
summary: Various media compile the best discounts on hardware, headphones, and devices during the shopping event.

title: Anthropic hires economist who accepts extinction risk and other news
summary: The AI startup hires Chad Jones, while Amazon tests Mythos on AWS and Lindy replaces Claude with Deepseek to save millions.

title: Elon Musk gets approval to acquire startup Mesh, settles FSD lawsuit, and premieres movie on X
summary: FTC approves Musk's purchase of Mesh, Tesla closes a FSD lawsuit, and the mogul releases a Uwe Boll movie on his network.

title: Video game news: Onimusha, Bloodborne on PC, Half-Life 2 in browser, and more
summary: Capcom announces date for Onimusha: Way of the Sword, ShadPS4 allows emulating Bloodborne with online, and Half-Life 2 can be played from the browser.

title: Multiple cyberattacks and security vulnerabilities reported
summary: From Russian malware to Linux exploits and flaws in Amazon Q Developer, experts warn of a wave of threats.

title: New hardware launches: ultrawide monitors, DDR4 memory, drivers, and processors
summary: INNOCN unveils a 49-inch monitor, GOODRAM launches DDR4, NVIDIA updates drivers for GTX, and Intel details its Nova Lake.

title: Samsung prepares Galaxy S27 and launches Galaxy A27 in Spain
summary: Four S27 series models appear in the GSMA database, while the A27 is already available with six years of updates.

title: Lenovo warns that RAM and SSD price increases will last until 2030
summary: The Chinese manufacturer warns about the sustained increase in components, while other analysts point out that it is the tip of the iceberg.
